text.title: caching-in-memory Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for caching-in-memory as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using lru_cache on a function that calls external I/O (DB, HTTP, filesystem).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: lru_cache decorates at import time and cannot be easily cleared by entity ID. When the underlying data changes, there is no invalidation path short of cache_clear() (which clears ALL entries). Use TTLCache with a short TTL instead so stale data expires automatically.

failure-mode.description: Using in-process L1 cache for user session state in a multi-worker deployment.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each worker has its own L1 cache. A session written by worker A is invisible to worker B. Sticky sessions mitigate this at the load-balancer level but break on worker restarts. Session state requires a shared store (Redis).
